content: We can reframe Self-Refine's single-perspective iterative self-optimization framework as a baseline that can be augmented by injecting multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hybrid self-refinement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or supervised data while fixing its blind spot of unrecognized self-bias. This hybrid system would let an LLM first generate an initial output, then pull conflicting critical perspectives from its own parametric knowledge to cross-evaluate its output across inconsistent worldviews before refining it, rather than only relying on the same initial perspective to generate feedback.
